summary: Iteration 9 P2 (Code Builder + Adzuna live + Data Lake builder_*) — full backend + frontend e2e validation completed successfully. Backend: 25/25 pytest cases pass in 32s (test_iter9_builder.py). Frontend: 8/8 UI steps pass on live preview URL. All builder endpoints working (create/list/get/preview/save/refine/share/delete/github status/github push). Adzuna live keys confirmed active — jobs endpoint returns source='adzuna' with 100+ real Indian tech jobs (first result: real company, not 'Zerodha/Razorpay/CRED' mock set). Cache behaviour verified: identical prompt returns cached=true credits_used=0 on 2nd call. Manual file edits are FREE (0 credits) and persist across GET. Refine deducts exactly 8 credits and returns modified files including 'IEMA.ai' footer text. Share endpoint returns valid S3 signed URL that responds HTTP 200 with HTML body; second share call reuses same share_key. GitHub error paths verified: no PAT → 400 'No GitHub PAT saved'; bad repo shape → 400 'owner/repo'; garbage PAT with valid-shape repo → does NOT 500 (returns 200 with errors[] populated). GitHub status transitions unconnected→connected after save_pat=true (encrypted PAT stored in users.github_pat), disconnect endpoint works. Preview composition correctly INLINES sibling script/link tags but PRESERVES external https:// URLs (cdn.tailwindcss.com stays as-is). Data Lake events verified: builder_create, builder_refine, builder_share all logged with correct user_id + payload. Regression: admin login, /auth/me, chat list, studio summarize, career learning-path all pass.

minor.issue: On subsequent share calls (share_key already exists), code calls upload_bytes with filename 'reupload.html' which generates a NEW random S3 key (see storage_service.upload_bytes L39: key=f'{folder}/{uuid.uuid4().hex}.{ext}'). This orphan object is uploaded but discarded — the returned signed URL is for the ORIGINAL share_key which still contains the STALE HTML from first share. Consequence: sharing after a refine will show OLD content. Fix: extract a lower-level `upload_bytes_at_key(key, data, ...)` that overwrites, or delete the else branch and always upload with a new key + update DB. Task spec says 'reuses same S3 key but refreshes signed URL' — the reuse-key part works but freshness is broken.

save_pat + garbage PAT is stored BEFORE the actual push attempt. If the PAT is garbage/invalid, /github/status will now report connected=true forever until user manually disconnects. Consider only saving PAT after at least one successful GitHub API call, OR validating the PAT via GET /user before saving.

issues: iframe sandbox has both 'allow-scripts' and 'allow-same-origin' which browser flags as escape-hatch security warning (see console). Consider dropping allow-same-origin unless truly required for the preview use case.

action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Fix share endpoint to actually re-upload fresh HTML to existing share_key (currently the reupload lands at an orphaned random key)
OPTIONAL/LOW: Validate GitHub PAT (GET /user) before persisting to avoid connected=true with a garbage token
OPTIONAL/LOW: Consider dropping allow-same-origin from preview iframe sandbox
critical_code_review_comments: builder_routes.share_project L191-199: `upload_bytes` in the else-branch does NOT overwrite the existing share_key — it always generates a new uuid key (storage_service.py L39). The returned signed URL points to the ORIGINAL key with stale content. This is a real bug where sharing after a refine returns yesterday's HTML. Add an `upload_bytes_at_key(key, data)` helper or unconditionally upload with a fresh key each call.

builder_routes.github_push L223-228: `save_pat` fires before the repo shape check AND before any real GitHub API call. A totally invalid PAT will be persisted (encrypted) and `/github/status` will lie about `connected=true`. Suggest gating save_pat on push success or on a lightweight PAT validation call (e.g., GET https://api.github.com/user).

builder_service.compose_preview_html L142: `by_path` keys use exact match on the file path. If LLM output uses `./index.html` or `/index.html` while a sibling `<link href="./style.css">` uses a different prefix, inlining silently no-ops. The current regex handles `./` stripping in the fallback, but not paths with subdirectories or with a leading slash. Consider path-normalization (posixpath.normpath) upfront.

builder_service._extract_json L50-67: robust to fences and stray prose, but ValueError is raised on parse failure with no user-friendly message — bubbles up as 500 from routes. Wrap the LlmChat call in the route with try/except and surface HTTP 502 'AI generator returned invalid JSON, please retry'.

builder_routes L26-27: Credit costs read from env at module import — hot-editing .env requires backend restart. Fine, but document it. Also, no `CREDIT_BUILDER_SHARE` env — share is free by design; consider making that explicit (0) so ops knows.

builder_service L22 hard-codes BUILDER_MODEL='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'; if a future model rev is desired, BUILDER_MODEL env exists — good. Just note the default is tied to a specific date-versioned model that may age out.

builder_routes.list_projects L88 uses `.limit(100)` with no pagination — for a user who creates many projects, they'll silently be capped. Add a `?limit`/`?offset` query param or return a `has_more` flag.

builder_routes.delete_project L166-171 swallows ALL exceptions and returns 200 — a user could get {ok:true} for a project that failed to delete (e.g., ObjectId cast failure). Tighten to just `except InvalidId: raise HTTPException(400)`.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter9_builder.py (new — 25 tests: builder create/list/get/preview/save/refine/share/delete + github status/push error paths + Adzuna live + data-lake events + regression)
seed_data_creation: Backend pytest: 1 unique builder project (`Simple TODO List App` variant) created + deleted at end; 1 S3-shared object under builder/{admin_user_id}/ folder (~9KB); 1 learning-path cache entry for role=TEST_Iter9_<hex>; wiped job_cache before Adzuna test. Frontend UI: 1 additional tic-tac-toe project left in admin's list (not deleted, harmless). Admin wallet was topped up with 200 bonus_credits mid-test because the backend pytest run + UI e2e (create+refine=23 credits) exceeded the default 100 welcome+daily+leftover balance. This is a one-time top-up; production wallet flow is unaffected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All P2 Code Builder features are working end-to-end. The 2 minor bugs (share stale-content on 2nd call + PAT-persisted-before-validation) are LOW priority and do not block acceptance. Adzuna live is confirmed operational (source='adzuna', 100+ real results, real company names). S3 share works and signed URLs are HTTP-200 reachable. GitHub error surface is clean (never 500s). If you re-run tests: (1) `pytest tests/test_iter9_builder.py -n 0` — parallel `-n 2 loadscope` breaks the shared project_id_holder fixture across workers; you MUST pass `-n 0` for serial. (2) The unique UNIQUE_PROMPT includes a uuid4 suffix so cache is fresh each run. (3) Cost per full run: ~23 credits (15 create + 8 refine). (4) Admin wallet was topped up to ~180 during this iteration; safe for one more full run.
rca of the issue: N/A — no test failures. Two low-severity code issues documented above are code-review observations, not test failures.
